idea-plugin.name: Equinox Colors
idea-plugin.description:
A carefully crafted color theme for developers who spend long hours in their IDE. Equinox Colors is built around two principles: ergonomic contrast and visual calm — every color pair meets WCAG AAA (7:1) contrast, and the palette avoids harsh saturation that strains the eyes over time.

Four variants, one cohesive system

  • Dark Modern — Unified dark background with an ergonomic slate-blue foundation. Every panel shares the same tone, creating a focused, distraction-free environment. Best for low-light or mixed-light environments.
  • Dark Contrast — A deeper editor sits inside a slightly lighter panel shell, giving the code area its own architectural weight. Good for those who like visual structure between editor and sidebar.
  • Light Soft — A warm cream-white background that replaces the harsh pure white of most light themes. Easy on the eyes in bright offices, near windows, or on high-brightness displays.
  • Light Contrast — Split light backgrounds with clear depth between editor and UI panels. Maximum legibility for daytime use or paper-like aesthetics.

Syntax palette

The syntax colors share the same cool-warm balance across all variants: soft violet keywords, slate blue functions, sage green strings, warm amber variables, dusty rose numbers, and muted comments that stay out of the way.

New UI & Islands

Each variant ships a *-islands.icls color scheme and a .theme.json UI theme for full compatibility with the New UI and the floating Islands panel layout introduced in IntelliJ IDEA 2024.1+.

Multi-platform

The same palette is available for VS Code and macOS Terminal. Find all versions at github.com/hlucas13/EquinoxTheme.

Installation

  1. Go to Settings → Plugins → Marketplace, search for Equinox Colors and click Install. Restart if prompted.
  2. Go to Settings → Appearance → Theme and select the Equinox variant you want (e.g. Equinox Dark Modern). This changes the IDE UI.
  3. Go to Settings → Editor → Color Scheme and select the matching Equinox scheme (same name as the theme you chose in step 2).
    Note: the editor color scheme does not change automatically when switching UI themes — this step is always required.

Islands layout (New UI): if you use the floating Islands panel layout, select the Equinox … Islands variant under Settings → Editor → Color Scheme for an optimised desktop background colour.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
• 1.0.1 — Clarified installation: editor color scheme must be set manually in Settings → Editor → Color Scheme after selecting the UI theme.
  • 1.0.0 — Initial release: 4 variants × classic ICLS + Islands ICLS + UI theme.json
